title: poa anceps subsp. polyphylla
intro: Poa anceps subsp. polyphylla, commonly known as the tall grass bluegrass, is a subspecies of grass native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Poa anceps subsp. polyphylla include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and ease congestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Poa anceps subsp. polyphylla include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by drying and processing the plant's leaves and stems.
